tactical_shape: 4-3-3 / attacking approach with young wide threats and a direct, pressing style
Faded after taking the lead and were forced into a halftime adjustment, with Amar Memic and Benjamin Tahirovic introduced at the break
standout_players: Kerim Alajbegovic — the goal of the game, an exciting 18-year-old who scored a beauty from 20 yards into the top corner
Edin Dzeko — involved in the second goal build-up, hit a post with a low effort, and reached his 150th cap
Ermin Mahmic — scored the crucial third goal to seal the win
Sead Kolasinac — one of only two players from the 2014 World Cup squad, part of the jubilant celebrations
fatigue_injuries: The side faded after the opening period, prompting halftime changes
home_read.momentum: Euphoric and through to the knockout stage; the win, and the third goal, boosted belief and confirmed progression as one of the best third-placed sides.

tactical_shape: Played some neat stuff going forward despite defensive vulnerability
Threatened from set pieces and quick combinations, including rehearsed corners and sharp passing around Akram Afif and Edmilson Junior
standout_players: Hassan al-Haydos — on his first World Cup start, scored Qatar’s goal
Pedro Miguel — almost equalised with a shot that hit the post after reading Akram Afif’s pass
Boualem Khoukhi — forced panic with a volley from a rehearsed corner
Akram Afif — provided a smart pass in the move that nearly brought an equaliser
away_read.momentum: Eliminated, but they showed more in attack than against Canada and briefly threatened Bosnia after the break.
